text.title: ADR Agentic Workflow
text.summary: Agent workflow for writing ADRs from spec/design context, ADR compliance review pattern, and gotchas specific to agent-generated decision records.

rule.statement: Before writing an ADR, the agent must read docs/adr/ to find the next sequential number, then read the relevant feature spec.md and design.md to source Context, Decision, and Consequences. Do not invent consequences not grounded in these documents.
rule.rationale: Invented consequences are the second most common quality failure after missing alternatives. Consequences must be traceable to real design tradeoffs, not generic technology wisdom.

rule.statement: After writing, set status to "Proposed" and stop. The agent must not proceed to Accepted without human review.
rule.rationale: Human sign-off is the primary quality gate for ADRs. Agents that self-approve skip the review where affected teams identify conflicts with other architectural constraints.

section.title: ADR Compliance Review
section.p: To audit whether accepted ADRs are still being followed, an agent reads docs/adr/, then inspects the codebase for each Accepted decision. Output: table of ADR number, status, "still followed?" (yes/no), and notes. Flag any violation as "review-needed".

rule.statement: Run ADR compliance review at major version bumps and after significant refactors. Mark obsolete decisions as "Deprecated" — do not leave orphaned Accepted ADRs that no longer reflect the codebase.
rule.rationale: Orphaned Accepted ADRs mislead agents and new team members. An ADR that says "We will use PostgreSQL" when the project now uses MongoDB is worse than no ADR.

antipattern.description: Agents fabricating alternatives in the Alternatives Considered section without grounding in real context.
antipattern.reason: Hallucinated alternatives undermine the credibility of the entire ADR. Always supply spec.md and design.md as input when asking an agent to write alternatives.

antipattern.description: Consequences section copied from the Decision section with different wording.
antipattern.reason: Consequences must describe downstream effects, not restate the decision. Require distinct, actionable consequence bullets in the prompt.

antipattern.description: Passing all 50+ ADR files to an agent instead of just the index list.
antipattern.reason: Full ADR history consumes 20-40k tokens. Pass only the index (NNN | Title | Status) and load individual ADRs on demand when their content is needed.
